response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive experience in software development, including roles involving C and C++ (National Instruments) and high-performance systems (Dunnhumby media), but his recent work has been heavily focused on web, mobile, and scripting languages (TypeScript, Node.js, Python) rather than C++ or industrial automation. His lack of recent C++ experience and no clear exposure to IEC 62443 or industrial cybersecurity standards raise concerns. However, his strong software engineering background, multi-threaded/concurrent programming knowledge, and open-source contributions could make him a potential fit with additional upskilling.
strengths: Strong background in C/C++ from early career (National Instruments, Dunnhumby media)
Experience with high-performance, low-latency systems and asynchronous I/O
Proven track record in software architecture, mentorship, and agile methodologies
concerns: Limited recent C++ and STL experience (last significant C++ work in 2015)
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443 or industrial cybersecurity standards
Lack of direct experience with industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
